THOUGHT: The miscompile occurs because InstCombine replaces copysign(x, y) with -fabs(x) based solely on the function’s nofpclass return attribute excluding positive classes. That deduction is invalid when NaNs are possible: copysign propagates the sign from the second operand even for NaNs, while -fabs(x) forces the sign to negative, causing a mismatch if y is positive and x is NaN. I will locate the copysign combine in InstCombineCalls.cpp and adjust it to avoid this transform unless we can prove the sign of the second operand (non-positive) and also rule out NaNs in the first operand, or otherwise disable the transform when NaNs may occur.
